headline_summary: IEA chief says Middle East oil supply disruptions will rise in April as Strait of Hormuz closure bites; diesel and jet fuel shortages flagged (Reuters, Apr 1)
Bahrain circulates revised UN Security Council draft on protecting shipping in/around Strait of Hormuz; China, Russia and France raise issues (Reuters, Apr 1)
US LNG exports hit record in March as Middle East war disrupts global supply and takes ~20% of global LNG supply offline (Reuters, Apr 1)
Trump says US will be out of Iran 'pretty quickly' and could return for 'spot hits'; NATO frictions noted (Reuters, Apr 1)
Dubai crude benchmark faces stress as Hormuz export halts disrupt deliverable grades and pricing mechanisms (Reuters, Apr 1)
Kenya tea exports hit as shipping disruption forces reroutes and leaves stocks piling up in Mombasa (Reuters, Apr 1)
Oil prices slide after Trump de-escalation comments, but uncertainty over reopening supply chains persists (Reuters, Apr 1)
OPEC oil output plunges in March as Hormuz closure forces export cuts, Reuters survey finds (Reuters, Mar 31)
Explosions heard in Damascus attributed by Syrian state TV to Israel intercepting Iranian missiles (Reuters, Mar 31)
Israel expands operations in southern Lebanon; Lebanon braces for long-term displacement crisis (Reuters, Mar 29-31)
risk_drivers: Strait of Hormuz remains effectively shut/near-halted; contested rules for safe passage
No UN consensus yet on authorizing maritime protection; escalation risk at sea remains elevated
Refined-products (diesel/jet) shortages and LNG outages add inflation and growth risks
Potential second chokepoint exposure at Bab al-Mandab if Yemen's Houthis target Red Sea shipping
Cross-border drone/missile incidents across Gulf/Levant keep tail-risk high for energy and logistics infrastructure
Benchmark/market-function stress (Dubai crude pricing) increases volatility and hedging dislocations

Energy: Crude and refined products retain upside risk premium while Hormuz is constrained; diesel/jet cracks and LNG spreads likely stay elevated (Reuters, Apr 1).
Volatility increases due to policy headlines (UN draft, US drawdown rhetoric) vs physical constraints (OPEC export cuts, benchmark disruption).
FX: Risk-off plus higher energy import bills can pressure oil-importer EM FX; USD tends to benefit during acute escalation (Reuters context: energy shock).
Rates: Energy-driven inflation risk supports breakevens; higher uncertainty can widen credit spreads and keep front-end rate cuts priced more cautiously.
Equities: Energy/defense beneficiaries vs airlines, shipping, chemicals and energy-intensive industries facing margin pressure; broader EM equity risk premium elevated.

sources.title: Middle East oil supply disruptions to rise in April and hit Europe, IEA chief says
Bahrain's Hormuz resolution runs into fresh obstacles at UN
US LNG exports break record high as Middle East war disrupts global supply
US to leave Iran 'pretty quickly' and return if needed, Trump tells Reuters
Iran war puts Middle East Dubai oil benchmark under stress as prices soar
Kenya tea exports hit by Iran conflict as stocks pile up
Oil slides after Trump says US will end its war on Iran soon
OPEC oil output plunges in March as war forces export cuts, Reuters survey finds

events.title: IEA warns Middle East oil disruptions will rise in April as Hormuz closure hits Europe
events.implication: April disruptions and refined fuel shortages raise inflation and growth risks, keeping energy risk premia elevated.

events.title: Bahrain circulates revised UN draft to protect Hormuz shipping; objections persist
events.location_name: United Nations Headquarters, New York, United States
events.implication: Lack of UN consensus delays multinational maritime security framework; confrontation risk at sea remains.

events.title: Dubai crude benchmark pricing strained as Hormuz export halts disrupt deliverable grades
events.implication: Benchmark dislocation increases volatility and basis risk for Asia-linked crude pricing and hedging.

events.title: US LNG exports hit record in March as Middle East outages and rerouting reshape flows
events.implication: Reduced Middle East LNG availability pulls incremental US cargoes to Europe/Asia, tightening global gas balances.

events.title: Trump says US will be out of Iran 'pretty quickly' and could return for 'spot hits'
events.implication: Headlines can compress risk premium short-term, but intermittent strikes keep uncertainty elevated.

events.title: OPEC output plunges in March as Hormuz closure forces export cuts
events.implication: Forced export cuts reduce effective supply and deepen backwardation/volatility in crude and products.

events.title: Explosions in Damascus attributed to Israel intercepting Iranian missiles, Syrian state TV says
events.implication: Air-defense activity underscores spillover pathways into Syria/Levant, raising escalation tail-risk.

events.title: Syrian army says drone attacks targeted bases near Iraq
events.implication: Persistent drone activity near the Iraq border increases risk to logistics corridors and US/allied assets.

events.title: Haifa oil refinery area hit by debris from intercepted missile; fire contained
events.implication: Even limited damage near refining assets can lift product risk premia and reinforces infrastructure vulnerability.

events.title: Israel expands southern Lebanon operations aiming for buffer zone to the Litani River
events.implication: Ground escalation increases regional spillover risk and complicates de-escalation pathways.

events.title: Lebanon prepares for long-term displacement crisis as fighting expands in the south
events.implication: Humanitarian strain raises political risk and potential for domestic unrest amid prolonged conflict.

events.title: Kenya tea exports hit as Middle East shipping disruption leaves stocks piling up in Mombasa
events.implication: Rerouting and surcharges disrupt trade flows and add to global goods inflation pressures.

events.title: Israeli strikes kill six in Gaza Strip, medics say
events.implication: Renewed violence adds another front of instability and complicates regional diplomacy.

events.title: Israel blocks Catholic cardinal from Holy Sepulchre on Palm Sunday, then eases access
events.implication: Security tightening in Jerusalem increases risk of localized unrest and international diplomatic friction.

events.title: Iranian authorities warned Bulgaria not to let US use airports, Bulgarian foreign ministry says
events.implication: Signals Iranian intent to deter allied basing/overflight support, raising NATO alliance-management risk.

links.from: IEA warns Middle East oil disruptions will rise in April as Hormuz closure hits Europe
links.why: IEA flags rising disruption and shortages, which typically increase crude/product risk premia.

links.from: OPEC output plunges in March as Hormuz closure forces export cuts
links.why: Lower OPEC output and forced export cuts reduce available barrels.

links.from: Dubai crude benchmark pricing strained as Hormuz export halts disrupt deliverable grades
links.why: Benchmark dysfunction increases hedging dislocations and can amplify price swings.

links.from: Bahrain circulates revised UN draft to protect Hormuz shipping; objections persist
links.to: IEA warns Middle East oil disruptions will rise in April as Hormuz closure hits Europe
links.why: UN divisions imply slower implementation of protections that could ease disruptions.

links.from: Trump says US will be out of Iran 'pretty quickly' and could return for 'spot hits'
links.why: De-escalation rhetoric can compress premiums, but conditional 'spot hits' keeps risk elevated.

links.from: US LNG exports hit record in March as Middle East outages and rerouting reshape flows
links.why: Higher gas prices and supply tightness can raise inflation uncertainty and pressure spreads.

links.from: Kenya tea exports hit as Middle East shipping disruption leaves stocks piling up in Mombasa
links.why: Shipping disruptions can weaken export receipts and add local inflation pressure in affected EMs.
Energy shocks often trigger broader risk-off positioning.
Risk-off typically widens EM risk premia and pressures EM FX.
Risk-off can widen corporate spreads and push safe-haven bid in rates.
